title: Variables
subtitle: Boxes for your data
goal: Learn what a variable is and why programmers use it.
explanation: Imagine a regular box. You can put anything inside it and take it out later.
In JavaScript, a variable plays the role of that box.
A variable has a name. Through that name, the program knows where the stored value lives.
Once you write a value, you can use it as many times as you need.
This lets programs remember information between different instructions.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: A variable is like a labeled box. The label on the outside says 'name', and 'Vitalii' is stored inside.

remember: You can get the value through its name.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think a variable is the value itself. In reality, it is a place where the value is stored.
